Will the terminals accept payments with just a tap for a total bill value greater than ₹5000? You can tap to pay for transactions greater than ₹5000, but you would be asked to enter PIN to complete the transaction. You can tap to pay for transactions greater than ₹5000, but you would be asked to enter PIN to complete the transaction. Will a charge slip be issued for all contactless transactions? Charge slips can be chosen to be issued for all transactions below ₹2000. For transactions above ₹2000, a charge slip will be provided automatically. Charge slips can be chosen to be issued for all transactions below ₹2000. For transactions above ₹2000, a charge slip will be provided automatically. If the customer were to get too close to the Visa contactless terminal, is there a risk of the card getting charged accidentally? No, the cashier will have to put in the amount in the terminal to activate the reader before the card can be tapped. Also, the card must be held within 4 cms of the contactless reader. No, the cashier will have to put in the amount in the terminal to activate the reader before the card can be tapped. Also, the card must be held within 4 cms of the contactless reader. If the customer were to tap their card more than once, would they be charged twice? No, the terminals are designed to make only one transaction per card at a time. No, the terminals are designed to make only one transaction per card at a time. If two contactless Visa cards are placed on the reader, would both the cards be charged? No. Either one of the two cards may be read depending on which card is closer and has a better antennae. The cashier can check which card has been charged by reading the last 4 digits of the card. However, one must always encourage the customer to tap only one card at a time. No. Either one of the two cards may be read depending on which card is closer and has a better antennae.
